behaviors.description: Storing a PASS review summary is blocked when any stored dimension finding for the same round has a FAIL verdict.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is gate logic + persisted findings lookup for the same round (module handoff), not just one pure function. Rejected higher levels: behavior only mentions "stored ... finding" and verdict checks; no real OS/network/subprocess, no AI output dependence, no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Wire summary-write path to a real local persistence layer (or in-memory DB adapter), seed round findings with at least one FAIL, attempt to store PASS summary, and assert write is rejected with no PASS state committed.

behaviors.description: Stop-gate verdict computation reads per-dimension stored findings directly and does not rely only on hand-written summary text.
behaviors.justification: Bug is specifically wrong data-source selection across modules (gate vs stored findings vs summary text). Rejected higher levels: behavior is about source-of-truth wiring, not OS/network/subprocess, not model judgment, not multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Persist findings containing a FAIL while providing contradictory summary text claiming PASS; run stop-gate computation and assert result follows stored findings (non-PASS) regardless of summary text.

behaviors.description: Manual summary text overrides require an explicit override flag and produce an auditable warning record.
behaviors.justification: This is policy enforcement plus audit-record side effect across components (override parser/validator + audit store). Rejected higher levels: behavior text requires explicit flag and warning record only; no real OS/network/subprocess, no AI output quality, no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise summary finalization through the real service layer and audit repository: (a) manual override without flag is rejected, (b) with explicit flag it is accepted and exactly one warning audit record is persisted with actor/round metadata.

behaviors.description: After Phase 4 fixes, the gate requires a review round identifier produced after the latest fix commit before PASS can be finalized.
behaviors.justification: Failure is recency/ordering validation across fix-commit metadata and review-round metadata. Rejected System: behavior says "requires ... identifier produced after the latest fix commit" and can be validated with stored commit/round timestamps or hashes without real git subprocess. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no dependence on model output content, only metadata ordering.
behaviors.test_description: Create fix-commit metadata and review-round records in local stores; assert PASS finalization fails when latest round predates the latest fix commit and succeeds only when round identifier is newer than that commit.

behaviors.description: In the live review loop, PASS cannot finalize unless a fresh review-agent round runs on the updated diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets orchestration/freshness gating (updated diff must have a subsequent round), which can be caught by module wiring and state transitions. Rejected Agentic: text requires that a round "runs on the updated diff" but does not require judging model output quality; this is infrastructure/orchestration around the call. Rejected Workflow: single freshness dependency, not multiple agentic decisions asserted.
behaviors.test_description: Run the review-loop orchestrator with real local state stores and a deterministic review-agent runner stub that records run inputs; update diff, attempt finalize without a new run (must fail), then run a fresh round on updated diff and assert finalize can proceed.

behaviors.description: If review rounds are exhausted with unresolved MUST-FIX findings in stored results, the outcome is escalation rather than PASS.
behaviors.justification: This is decision logic over persisted round state and finding severity (MUST-FIX) at exhaustion boundary. Rejected higher levels: behavior explicitly anchors on "stored results" and outcome mapping; no real OS/network/subprocess, no model-quality assertion, no required multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Seed stored round results to exhausted state with unresolved MUST-FIX findings, execute outcome resolver, and assert result is escalation and PASS is blocked.

behaviors.description: In a live multi-round review workflow, exhausted rounds with unresolved MUST-FIX findings still block PASS and emit escalation.
behaviors.justification: Even in "live multi-round" wording, asserted failure mode is terminal policy + escalation emission when stored unresolved MUST-FIX remains. Rejected Workflow: behavior does not require validating multiple model decisions, only final gate/escalation behavior at exhaustion. Rejected Agentic/System: no required model-output quality check or OS/network/subprocess dependency stated.
behaviors.test_description: Execute multi-round orchestrator path with local persisted round states through exhaustion, ensuring unresolved MUST-FIX remains; assert finalization stays blocked and escalation event/record is emitted.
